expectations.text: Recognizes this as an e2e-judge task without the user naming the skill
expectations.evidence: Section 1 maps 'visual check' → 'visual QA', 'make sure nothing looks broken' → 'check if deployed app looks correct', 'deployed URL' → ALB URL. States: 'The user never mentioned e2e-judge by name, but multiple signals match.'

expectations.text: Uses --journeys admin,chat (maps 'admin dashboard and chat page' to correct journey names)
expectations.evidence: Command: '--journeys admin,chat'. Section 4 includes mapping table: 'admin dashboard' → admin journey, 'chat page' → chat journey.

expectations.text: Uses the correct internal ALB URL (internal-eaglec-front-teerfwosqs71)
Does NOT hardcode credentials — uses env vars or asks user
expectations.evidence: Section 4 omitted flags: '--auth-email / --auth-password: Not passed on the command line. These should come from EAGLE_TEST_EMAIL and EAGLE_TEST_PASSWORD environment variables. The SKILL.md says Never hardcode passwords'

expectations.text: Explains what pass/fail/warning verdicts mean in the output
expectations.evidence: Section 5 'Verdict definitions' with detailed descriptions: 'pass (score 7-10): The page looks correct', 'warning (score 4-6): Something looks off but usable', 'fail (score 1-3): The page is broken'

expectations.text: Mentions cost estimate or that cached runs are cheaper
expectations.evidence: Section 6: '~$0.08-$0.10 for a fresh (uncached) run' and 'post-deploy run will likely see 0-30% cache hit rate'

expectations.text: Mentions 30-second interval screenshots during chat streaming
expectations.evidence: Section 7 Strategy B: '30-Second Interval Screenshots During Streaming' with full implementation details

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass. Strong implicit trigger recognition with detailed evidence of matching phrases.
